zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Stevens於1540年出生在英國英格蘭倫敦聖 波托爾福也斯 邱奇 阿爾蓋特時，他的父親Henry Stephens40歲，母親Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand50歲。他於1568年12月1日與Elizabeth Andrewe在英國米德萊可倫敦 聖 波托爾福也斯 阿爾蓋特結婚。他去世於1617年1月在英國奧可福爾卡維沙安享年77歲。

nameMeanings.name: Stevens
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (London), Flemish, Dutch, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Steven . The surname of Flemish origin is also found in the Walloon part of Belgium. In North America, the English form of the surname has also absorbed some like-sounding Jewish names and various other European cognates, e.g. Greek Stefanidis , Serbian Stevanović (see Stevanovic ), Slovenian and Slovak Štefanič (see Stefanic ).

variants.name: Stephans
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of North German Steffans, a patronymic from Steffan , or of its cognate Steffens .

variants.name: Steeves
meanings: English (Lancashire): from a shortened form of   Stevens  . Compare   Steveson  .

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Scottish, English, Dutch, and North German: from the personal name Steven, from Latin Stephanus, Greek Stephanos ‘crown’. This was a popular name throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages, having been borne by the first Christian martyr, stoned to death at Jerusalem three years after the death of Christ. In North America, this surname has also absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. German Steffen , German and Polish Stefan , Slovenian and Slovak Štefan, Hungarian István (see Istvan ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see Stevens ).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Stevens was born in 1540, in Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, London, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry Stephens, was 40 and his mother, Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand, was 50. He married Elizabeth Andrewe on 1 December 1568, in London Saint Botolph's Aldgate, Middlesex, England. He died in January 1617, in Caversham, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 77.

ko.Saint Botolph's Church Aldgate, London,: 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 처르치 알드가트
ko.London Saint Botolph's Aldgate, Middlese: 영국 미들레세스 런던 세인트 벗프'스 알드가트
ko.lifeSummary: Richard Stevens(이)가 1540년 영국 잉글랜드 런던 세인트 벗프'스 처르치 알드가트에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry Stephens(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand(은)는 50세였다. 그는 1568년 12월 1일 영국 미들레세스 런던 세인트 벗프'스 알드가트에서 Elizabeth Andrewe(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1617년 1월 영국 스퍼드실르 카버샤에서 77세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Stevens родился в 1540 г. в местности сеинт ботолф'с чурч алджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry Stephens было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibbell Frances Campyn Marchand, 50. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Andrewe 1 декабря 1568 г. в местности лондон сеинт ботолф'с алджэит, миддлесекс, Англия. Он умер январь 1617 г. в местности кавиршам, оксфордшир, Англия в возрасте 77 лет.
